blurb: Delivers emergency, hospital, and surgical care to Albertans — overseeing acute care facilities, surgical wait times, cancer care, organ and tissue donation, health system quality, and patient advocacy through the Office of the Alberta Health Advocates.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Albertans can access timely hospital and surgical care
goals.description: The ministry ensures Albertans can access emergency departments, hospital care, and timely surgical and diagnostic procedures across the province.

foundational_services.name: Plan and fund hospital and surgical capacity across Alberta
foundational_services.desc: Develops provincial plans for hospital capacity, surgical volumes, and emergency services. Works with health entities to manage bed capacity, operating room schedules, and emergency department resources to meet population demand.

main_services.name: Access emergency and hospital care information

supporting.name: Find a hospital or emergency department
supporting.desc: Albertans can locate hospitals, urgent care centres, and emergency departments across the province, including facility details, services offered, and contact information.
supporting.users: All Albertans needing emergency or hospital care

reach_estimate.estimate: Millions of emergency visits and hospital admissions annually
reach_estimate.basis: All Albertans may need emergency or hospital care at any time

economic_impact.note: Emergency and acute care is the safety net of the health system

supporting.name: Check surgical and diagnostic wait times
supporting.desc: Albertans can view estimated wait times for surgeries and diagnostic procedures by procedure type and location. Helps patients and physicians plan care and manage expectations.
supporting.users: Patients waiting for procedures, referring physicians

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of searches annually
reach_estimate.basis: High public interest in wait time information for surgical and diagnostic care

economic_impact.note: Transparency supports informed patient choice and system accountability

main_services.name: Access cancer care and organ donation services

supporting.name: Learn about cancer care programs and find treatment centres
supporting.desc: Alberta provides comprehensive cancer care through regional cancer centres and community oncology clinics. Albertans can find treatment locations, learn about screening programs, and access information on cancer prevention, diagnosis, and treatment.

reach_estimate.estimate: Over 20,000 new cancer diagnoses in Alberta annually
reach_estimate.basis: Cancer is among the most common health conditions requiring specialized hospital care

economic_impact.note: Timely cancer care directly affects survival and quality of life

supporting.name: Register as an organ and tissue donor
supporting.desc: Albertans can register their decision to be an organ and tissue donor. The provincial organ and tissue donation program coordinates donation and transplantation services across Alberta's hospitals.

reach_estimate.estimate: Over 1 million registered donors in Alberta
reach_estimate.basis: Ongoing public campaigns to increase donor registration

economic_impact.note: Organ donation saves lives and reduces the cost of long-term therapies like dialysis

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Health system quality and safety are maintained
goals.description: The ministry oversees health system quality, patient safety, health data and research, and provides independent advocacy for Albertans with health system concerns.

foundational_services.name: Develop health system policy, standards, and performance measurement
foundational_services.desc: Sets health system standards, develops performance measures, commissions health research, and publishes health system data to support evidence-based policy making and continuous quality improvement across the health system.

main_services.name: Access health data, research, and system performance information

supporting.name: Access health research data and apply for research funding
supporting.desc: The ministry supports health research through data access, funding programs, and partnerships. Researchers can access de-identified health data and apply for provincial health research grants.
supporting.users: Health researchers, academic institutions, health system planners

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of research applications and data requests annually
reach_estimate.basis: Specialized audience of health researchers and academics

economic_impact.note: Health research drives innovation, improves care quality, and attracts research investment

supporting.name: Report a patient safety concern at a health facility
supporting.desc: Albertans can report patient safety concerns, infection control issues, and quality of care problems at hospitals and health facilities. Reports support continuous quality improvement and regulatory oversight.
supporting.users: Patients, family members, health care workers

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of patient safety reports annually
reach_estimate.basis: Provincial oversight of all hospital and health facility operations

economic_impact.note: Safety reporting drives quality improvement and prevents future incidents

main_services.name: Get help from the Health Advocate with a health system concern

supporting.name: Contact the Health Advocate for help with a concern
supporting.desc: The Office of the Alberta Health Advocates provides independent assistance to Albertans who have concerns about health services, hospital care, or mental health and addiction services. Advocates help navigate the system and resolve complaints.
supporting.users: Patients, family members, caregivers with health system concerns

reach_estimate.basis: Independent advocacy office serving Albertans with health system concerns

economic_impact.note: Provides independent complaint resolution and systemic recommendations for improvement
